tree.setup: A corner office on the eighth floor of a design firm, where someone has placed a small bamboo plant on every windowsill. Ms. Chen Mei, the hiring manager, interviews candidates the way a gardener evaluates seedlings — not for what they are now, but for what they might become. She offers you tea before a single question.
tree.setup_zh: 一家设计公司八楼的角落办公室，每个窗台上都摆着一盆小竹子。招聘经理陈梅面试候选人就像园丁挑选树苗——看的不是现在，而是将来。她在提问之前先给你倒了茶。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 请坐，先喝口茶。你可以先自我介绍一下吗？
turns.text_pinyin: qǐng zuò, xiān hē kǒu chá. nǐ kěyǐ xiān zìwǒ jièshào yíxià ma?
turns.text_en: Please sit down and have some tea first. Could you introduce yourself?

options.text_zh: 谢谢。我叫……，去年从大学毕业，学的是设计。我对你们公司的项目很感兴趣。
options.text_pinyin: xièxie. wǒ jiào……, qùnián cóng dàxué bìyè, xué de shì shèjì. wǒ duì nǐmen gōngsī de xiàngmù hěn gǎn xìngqù.
options.text_en: Thank you. My name is... I graduated from university last year, majoring in design. I'm very interested in your company's projects.
options.feedback: Structured and warm. Ms. Chen sets down her pen — she's listening rather than just taking notes.

options.text_zh: 我是设计专业的，想来你们公司工作。
options.text_pinyin: wǒ shì shèjì zhuānyè de, xiǎng lái nǐmen gōngsī gōngzuò.
options.text_en: I studied design, and I want to work at your company.
options.feedback: Direct but a bit thin. She'll want to know more, but the honesty is refreshing.

options.text_zh: 嗯……我不太会自我介绍。我很喜欢设计。
options.text_pinyin: ǹg……wǒ bú tài huì zìwǒ jièshào. wǒ hěn xǐhuān shèjì.
options.text_en: Um... I'm not great at introducing myself. I really like design.
options.feedback: The honesty is endearing. Ms. Chen smiles — she was once equally nervous in this exact chair.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 很好。你以前有没有工作经验？实习也算。
turns.text_pinyin: hěn hǎo. nǐ yǐqián yǒu méiyǒu gōngzuò jīngyàn? shíxí yě suàn.
turns.text_en: Good. Do you have any work experience? Internships count too.

options.text_zh: 我在一家小公司实习过半年，主要做网页设计。学到了很多实际的东西。
options.text_pinyin: wǒ zài yì jiā xiǎo gōngsī shíxí guò bàn nián, zhǔyào zuò wǎngyè shèjì. xué dào le hěn duō shíjì de dōngxi.
options.text_en: I interned at a small company for six months, mainly doing web design. I learned a lot of practical things.
options.feedback: Specific and grounded. She makes a small note: 「实践经验。」
True but vague. She'll ask for details, which is fine — she's patient.

options.text_zh: 没有正式的工作经验，但是我在学校做过很多项目。
options.text_pinyin: méiyǒu zhèngshì de gōngzuò jīngyàn, dànshì wǒ zài xuéxiào zuò guò hěn duō xiàngmù.
options.text_en: No formal work experience, but I did many projects at school.
options.feedback: Honest. Ms. Chen respects that more than embellishment — she's seen enough decorated resumes.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不错。你觉得你最大的优点是什么？
turns.text_pinyin: búcuò. nǐ juéde nǐ zuìdà de yōudiǎn shì shénme?
turns.text_en: Not bad. What do you think is your greatest strength?

options.text_zh: 我觉得我比较认真，而且愿意学新的东西。遇到问题不会轻易放弃。
options.text_pinyin: wǒ juéde wǒ bǐjiào rènzhēn, érqiě yuànyì xué xīn de dōngxi. yùdào wèntí bú huì qīngyì fàngqì.
options.text_en: I think I'm quite conscientious, and willing to learn new things. I don't give up easily when facing problems.
options.feedback: 认真 and persistence — the bamboo-plant qualities Ms. Chen looks for. She nods slowly.
Everyone says this, but the way you say it — without bravado — makes her believe you.

options.text_zh: 我不确定……可能是我的创意？
options.text_pinyin: wǒ bú quèdìng……kěnéng shì wǒ de chuàngyì?
options.text_en: I'm not sure... maybe my creativity?
options.feedback: The question mark at the end is almost audible. But 创意 is exactly what a design firm needs — own it more next time.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 最后一个问题——你有什么想问我的吗？
turns.text_pinyin: zuìhòu yí gè wèntí — nǐ yǒu shénme xiǎng wèn wǒ de ma?
turns.text_en: Last question — is there anything you'd like to ask me?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Ask a thoughtful question about the company.

options.text_zh: 请问新员工一般会参加什么样的培训？我想知道公司怎么帮助新人成长。
options.text_pinyin: qǐngwèn xīn yuángōng yìbān huì cānjiā shénme yàng de péixùn? wǒ xiǎng zhīdào gōngsī zěnme bāngzhù xīnrén chéngzhǎng.
options.text_en: What kind of training do new employees usually receive? I'd like to know how the company helps newcomers grow.
options.feedback: A question about growth — Ms. Chen's favorite topic. The bamboo on the windowsill seems to lean toward you approvingly.

options.text_zh: 请问上班时间是几点到几点？
options.text_pinyin: qǐngwèn shàngbān shíjiān shì jǐ diǎn dào jǐ diǎn?
options.feedback: Practical, but she was hoping for something that showed more curiosity about the work itself.
An interview without questions is like a garden without rain. Ms. Chen writes a small note, but her smile doesn't waver.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好问题。我们有三个月的培训计划。谢谢你来面试，我们会尽快通知你结果的。
turns.text_pinyin: hǎo wèntí. wǒmen yǒu sān gè yuè de péixùn jìhuà. xièxie nǐ lái miànshì, wǒmen huì jǐnkuài tōngzhī nǐ jiéguǒ de.
turns.text_en: Good question. We have a three-month training program. Thank you for coming — we'll notify you of the result as soon as possible.

options.text_zh: 谢谢您，陈经理。今天跟您聊得很愉快，期待您的回复。
options.text_pinyin: xièxie nín, Chén jīnglǐ. jīntiān gēn nín liáo de hěn yúkuài, qīdài nín de huífù.
options.text_en: Thank you, Manager Chen. I enjoyed our conversation today and look forward to your reply.
options.feedback: Graceful exit. Ms. Chen watches you leave and waters the nearest bamboo — her way of saying the interview went well.
Polite and clean. She files your resume near the top of the stack.

options.text_pinyin: hǎo de, nà wǒ zǒu le.
options.feedback: A bit abrupt for a formal setting, but Ms. Chen is forgiving. The bamboo, however, is not.

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese interviews, it's common for the interviewer to offer tea (请喝茶) as a gesture of hospitality. Asking about 培训 (péixùn, training) shows you're thinking long-term, which employers value highly.
